text: If you like ambient music, Siddharth Khosla's film scores are incredible background listening.
label: recommendation

text: Agam is what you listen to when you want prog rock with Carnatic influences. Start with Mahaganapathim.
label: recommendation

text: For Hindustani classical beginners, Kishori Amonkar's Bhairavi recordings are the perfect entry point.
label: recommendation

text: Dharohar Project does folk fusion really well, especially their Rajasthani stuff.
label: recommendation

text: Check out Easy Wanderlings if you want dreamy indie pop with clean English lyrics.
label: recommendation

text: Mali is criminally underrated for Indian indie in English, start with his EP Suno.
label: recommendation

text: Bombay Bicycle Club's Flaws album is perfect if you want something stripped down and acoustic.
label: recommendation

text: If you loved Dil Se soundtrack, go straight to Mani Ratnam's entire AR Rahman collaboration catalog.
label: recommendation

text: I disagree that AR Rahman has declined. Rockstar and Highway both have incredible scores that people sleep on.
label: argument

text: You cant compare Arijit Singh to Mohd Rafi, they serve completely different functions in their eras.
label: argument

text: Calling vinyl nostalgia is missing the point. The ritual of listening matters, not just the sound quality.
label: argument

text: I actually think the remix culture in Bollywood isnt all bad. It introduces older songs to younger audiences.
label: argument

text: Respectfully disagree about The Local Train. Their Khairiyat era was genuinely fresh for Indian indie.
label: argument

text: The AI music debate is not comparable to DAW debates because DAWs still require human musical decisions at every step.
label: argument

text: People saying Olivia Rodrigo is all PR are ignoring that SOUR was written almost entirely by her at 17.
label: argument

text: I hear what youre saying about pitch correction but some artists use it as an intentional aesthetic, not a crutch.
label: argument

text: Streaming hasnt ruined music discovery, it has democratized it. Small artists can now reach global audiences without labels.
label: argument

text: Saying someone has bad taste for preferring GUTS over SOUR is exactly the kind of gatekeeping that drives people away from music communities.
label: argument
